content.problemStatement:
Chef and some of his friends are planning to participate in a puzzle hunt event.

The rules of the puzzle hunt state:

"This hunt is intended for teams of 666 to 888 people."

Chef's team has NNN people in total. Are they eligible to participate?


Input Format
The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN: the number of people present in Chef's team.



Output Format
Print the answer: `Yes` if Chef's team is eligible to participate, and `No` otherwise.


Each letter in the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the outputs `NO`, `No`, `nO`, `no` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the size of Chef's team.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "Yes" if 666 ≤ N ≤ 888, otherwise print "No". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: N=4 is less than 666, so the team is not eligible.
N=777 lies within the required range [666, 888]; the team is eligible.
N=888 is the upper bound of the allowed range, so the answer is Yes.

solution.approach: Read T. For each test case read N and check whether it lies in the inclusive interval [666, 888]. Output Yes if true, otherwise No. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
